sharedGenerationSettings.sourceExpectation: native high-resolution individual sprite source, 4096 px long edge minimum when available
sharedGenerationSettings.background: transparent or flat neutral extraction-safe background
sharedGenerationSettings.framing: full body, complete hands, complete feet, complete props, 10-14 percent safe padding
sharedGenerationSettings.style: premium clean 2.5D game-sprite raster material, warm human soft Santa concierge identity, lived-in older face, slight belly, natural asymmetry

poseRows.passiveProp: ivory keycard half-tucked into breast pocket or ledger band
poseRows.handSafety: Both hands relaxed and visible; no prop grip required.
poseRows.composition: Props must be costume-level accents only; Otis's belly curve and welcoming stance are the read.

poseRows.handSafety: Keycard pinched flat between thumb and index finger; other hand open palm. Do not curl fingers through loops.
poseRows.composition: Card extends slightly forward but stays below beard and within shoulder width.

poseRows.handSafety: Closed ledger cradled against side ribs; fingers mostly hidden behind one edge, thumb visible.
poseRows.composition: Ledger sits beside belly, not across it; face and listening expression remain dominant.

poseRows.handSafety: Ledger tucked under one forearm; free hand near chin with relaxed fingers, not touching prop.
poseRows.composition: Use asymmetrical shoulders and slight belly; prop is a quiet memory object.

poseRows.passiveProp: ledger tucked low at opposite hip only if it does not crowd silhouette
poseRows.handSafety: One open explaining hand; keycard held flat at chest-lower-rib height with simple pinch.
poseRows.composition: Card supports the explanation, not a salesman pose.

poseRows.handSafety: Bell rests on tiny half-moon threshold ledge; one index finger presses plunger. No hand wraps around bell.
poseRows.composition: The bell is readable but small; Otis's face remains calm, protective, and human.

poseRows.passiveProp: aged-brass-service-bell resting on far side of ledge only if it stays tiny
poseRows.handSafety: Tablet folio supported against forearm like a tray; no two-thumb typing unless separately approved.
poseRows.composition: Screen stays dark and abstract; Otis is operating the Tower, not becoming a tech-dashboard mascot.

outfitSlots.description: Approved classic soft Santa concierge outfit; warm jacket, tailored but comfortable, subtle brass trim.
Slightly more formal threshold-keeper variant; deeper jacket, premium lapel, still lived-in and approachable.
Practical working variant; refined cardigan or service jacket, sleeves and materials suited to tablet/ledger work.

shots.primaryProbeQuestion: Can the keycard be a tiny access cue without cluttering the base identity?
Can a flat keycard make the threshold invitation readable while preserving hand anatomy?
Can the ledger support empathy without covering the belly or face?
Can a tucked ledger read as memory instead of clutter?
Can the keycard support explanation without becoming a sales badge?
Can the bell live on a ledge so fingers stay clean?
Can the tablet folio feel operational while staying warm and low-detail?
Does a passive keycard still read with the more formal outfit?
Does the formal outfit make the keycard too hotel-staff literal?
Does the ledger preserve warmth against a more formal costume?
Does the tucked ledger keep the premium silhouette intact?
Can the keycard stay permission-coded rather than luxury-doorman coded?
Does the bell become too ceremonial with the formal outfit?
Does the tablet folio modernize the formal look without breaking canon?
Can the working outfit carry passive access cues without busy pockets?
Does the keycard remain warm and personal in a practical outfit?
Can ledger texture keep the working outfit from becoming generic tech support?
Does the ledger-tuck produce a clean mobile silhouette?
Can the keycard and working outfit avoid badge clutter?
Does the ledge bell still feel premium in the practical variant?
Can tablet, bell, and belly all coexist without turning into a desk scene?
